rows.notes: Top-centre page title is clearly legible: 'Receipts anni presentis. 1789.' Vertical ruling lines not used for this header.

rows.description: Kidlingh. Reck. pro an. ad. Quist
rows.notes: Numbers (93 15 0) are clearly aligned with the pounds/shillings/pence columns; vertical ruling gives unambiguous column placement. Description handwriting is partially ambiguous — first word reads as 'Kidlingh' and 'Reck.' is likely 'Reck.' (receipt/rect.), trailing words faint; chosen reading based on letter shapes and common ledger phrasing.
rows.english_description: Annual rent received from the Kidlington estate (quit/rack rent).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual rent received from the Kidlington estate, amounting to £93 15s 0d.

rows.description: Gwin. Rest. pro dim. an. ad. f. Currence
rows.notes: Amount 30 0 0 is clear and validates against column boundaries. Description is moderately unclear: first token looks like 'Gwin.' or 'Gwin.', next part 'Rest. pro dim. an.' (pro dimidiate annu?); the final word is uncertain — transcribed conservatively from handwriting shapes.
rows.english_description: Half-yearly rent described as from 'Gwin' (name uncertain).
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-yearly rent described as from 'Gwin' (name uncertain), amounting to £30 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount 03 0 0 is clear and columns well aligned. Description reads plausibly as 'Fetherston pro dim. an.' — 'Fetherston' letters reasonably legible though final letters slightly cramped.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Half-yearly rent from Fetherston, amounting to £3 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount 06 0 0 is clear and well aligned. Description 'Back Gate Coppin pro an.' is legible and fits common place/tenant naming conventions in the ledger.
rows.english_description: Annual rent from the Back Gate Coppin property.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annual rent from the Back Gate Coppin property, amounting to £6 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount 20 0 0 is clear. Description is fairly uncertain: letters read as 'Menchinstor' or similar; 'Reck.' and 'pro annos' (pro annis/for the year) are readable but the proper place/name is ambiguous due to flourished handwriting.
rows.english_description: Rent from Menchinstor covering multiple years.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rent from Menchinstor covering multiple years, amounting to £20 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Amount 04 0 0 is clear and columns align. Description is partly unclear: first word appears as 'Kidlin' or 'Kidlinh', second as 'Vie' (might be 'Vie' or 'Vic'), interpreted as 'Kidlin Vie pro annis' based on visible letter forms.
rows.english_description: Rents from Kidlington covering multiple years.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Rents from Kidlington covering multiple years, amounting to £4 0s 0d.

rows.notes: Horizontal rule followed by total '156 15 0' is clear. Sum matches the listed pound/shilling amounts above (93+30+3+6+20+4 = 156 pounds; shillings sum 15). Column alignment unambiguous.
Section header 'Bankers Account' is clearly written and underlined; no amounts on this header row.

rows.description: Packeager Annuity for a year from Midst. 1788 to Midst. 1789
rows.notes: Amount 50 0 0 is clearly written and aligned. Description mostly readable though the first word could be 'Packeager' or 'Packager' — chosen 'Packeager' to reflect the visible 'k' and 'ea' letter shapes; the date range 'Midst. 1788 to Midst. 1789' is legible.
rows.english_description: Annuity (Packeager) payable for the year from Midsummer 1788 to Midsummer 1789.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Annuity (Packeager) payable for the year from Midsummer 1788 to Midsummer 1789, amounting to £50 0s 0d.

rows.description: Interest of 1714. at 3 per cent. for the year as above
rows.notes: Amount 51 8 4 is clear and fits into the columns without ambiguity. Description 'Interest of 1714. at 3 per cent. for the year as above' is legible; small flourish on '1714' is unambiguous.
rows.english_description: Interest at 3% on the 1714 fund for the stated year.
rows.english_description_with_amount: Interest at 3% on the 1714 fund for the stated year, amounting to £51 8s 4d.

rows.notes: Final subtotal '101 8 4' under a horizontal rule is clearly written. Arithmetic check: 50 + 51 = 101; shillings and pence correspond to the summed entries. Column alignment is clear.
